cards.title: The 'AI-Friendly Web' You Can Build Today: Beat Information Overload for Builders
cards.thesis: The explosion of AI is causing serious information overload for builders, who are actively looking for simpler ways to consume information and streamline their development workflows. People are so desperate for clean, AI-digestible content that they're manually trying to find simplified versions of websites, signaling a huge unmet need for tools that cut through the noise.

evidence.text: People want 'modern tooling' for building apps with AI, from 'motivated newbie' to existing developers who want to get up to speed.
Users are tired of the 'marketing heavy web' and manually adding `/llm.txt` to URLs because they find 'content for LLMs strait to the point and clear,' even though browsers don't render it well.
Developers struggle with 'too much raw information' and 'overload' when trying to keep up with new ideas in AI, frontend, backend, and other areas.

cards.opportunity: The fact that people are manually adding `/llm.txt` to URLs indicates a strong, unmet need for simplified, AI-friendly web content that cuts through the 'marketing heavy web.' Instead of waiting for websites to offer it, you could ship a browser extension *this weekend* that automatically scrapes a page, strips out marketing fluff, and presents a clean, markdown-like summary. This immediately solves the 'information overload' problem for builders trying to feed clean data to their AI tools or just understand complex topics quickly.
